entry.name: ADJACENT
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for slot positions adjacent to a form control (clear, prepend, append, appendInner, prependInner).
entry.definition:
export enum ADJACENT {
    CLEAR = 'clear',
    PREPEND = 'prepend',
    APPEND = 'append',
    APPEND_INNER = 'appendInner',
    PREPEND_INNER = 'prependInner'
}

values.description_fallback: Clear action slot — renders a dismiss / clear button.
Outer prepend slot — rendered before the control, outside the input wrapper.
Outer append slot — rendered after the control, outside the input wrapper.
Inner append slot — rendered inside the input wrapper, trailing edge.
Inner prepend slot — rendered inside the input wrapper, leading edge.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { ADJACENT } from 'origam'

const position: ADJACENT = ADJACENT.APPEND_INNER

entry.name: ALIGN
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for cross-axis alignment in flex/grid layouts (start, end, center, baseline, stretch).
entry.definition:
export enum ALIGN {
    START = 'start',
    END = 'end',
    CENTER = 'center',
    BASELINE = 'baseline',
    STRETCH = 'stretch'
}

values.description_fallback: Align items to the start of the cross axis.
Align items to the end of the cross axis.
Center items on the cross axis.
Align items along their text baseline.
Stretch items to fill the cross axis.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { ALIGN } from 'origam'

const myAlign: ALIGN = ALIGN.CENTER

entry.name: AUDIO_LOOP_MODE
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for the loop strategy of OrigamAudio when a playlist is active (none, all, one).

values.description_fallback: No loop — playback stops when the last track ends.
Loop the whole playlist — wraps back to the first track after the last.
Loop the current track — restarts the same track at 0 when it ends.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { AUDIO_LOOP_MODE } from 'origam'

const loop: AUDIO_LOOP_MODE = AUDIO_LOOP_MODE.ALL

entry.name: AUDIO_VARIANT
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for the visual variant of OrigamAudio (expanded, compact). Deprecated aliases: normal, minimal.

values.description_fallback: Full studio strip: 96px album cover, title/artist/album header, waveform scrubber and transport row.
Slim transport-only dock: 48px cover, single inline metadata strip, transport row only.
Deprecated alias for EXPANDED — same runtime value.
Deprecated alias for COMPACT — same runtime value.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { AUDIO_VARIANT } from 'origam'

const variant: AUDIO_VARIANT = AUDIO_VARIANT.COMPACT

entry.name: AXIS
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for the drag or scroll axis constraint (both, x, y).

values.description_fallback: Unconstrained — movement allowed on both X and Y axes.
Constrained to the horizontal axis only.
Constrained to the vertical axis only.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { AXIS } from 'origam'

const dragAxis: AXIS = AXIS.X

entry.name: BLOCK
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for block-axis anchor positions used in overlay and floating element placement (top, bottom).

values.description_fallback: Anchor or position at the top of the block axis.
Anchor or position at the bottom of the block axis.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { BLOCK } from 'origam'

const anchor: BLOCK = BLOCK.BOTTOM

entry.name: BLOCKQUOTE_LANG
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for the language of a blockquote — controls locale-aware quotation marks and hyphenation (fr, en, es, de, auto).
entry.definition:
export enum BLOCKQUOTE_LANG {
    FR = 'fr',
    EN = 'en',
    ES = 'es',
    DE = 'de',
    AUTO = 'auto'
}

values.description_fallback: French — uses « guillemets » and French typographic conventions.
English — uses "curly double quotes" and English conventions.
Spanish — uses «comillas angulares» and Spanish conventions.
German — uses „Anführungszeichen" and German conventions.
Auto — inherits the document language attribute.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { BLOCKQUOTE_LANG } from 'origam'

const lang: BLOCKQUOTE_LANG = BLOCKQUOTE_LANG.FR

entry.name: BLOCKQUOTE_VARIANT
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for the visual style of a blockquote (default, elegant, quoted, minimal, pull).
entry.definition:
export enum BLOCKQUOTE_VARIANT {
    DEFAULT = 'default',
    ELEGANT = 'elegant',
    QUOTED = 'quoted',
    MINIMAL = 'minimal',
    PULL = 'pull'
}

values.description_fallback: Standard left-border blockquote styling.
Refined typographic style with decorative top and bottom rules.
Large decorative quotation marks framing the content.
Minimal chrome — no border, subtle text weight contrast only.
Pull-quote style — enlarged text floated within a long-form article.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { BLOCKQUOTE_VARIANT } from 'origam'

const variant: BLOCKQUOTE_VARIANT = BLOCKQUOTE_VARIANT.ELEGANT

entry.name: BORDER_STYLE
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum mirroring CSS border-style values (none, hidden, dotted, dashed, solid, double, groove, ridge, inset, outset).
entry.definition:
export enum BORDER_STYLE {
    NONE = 'none',
    HIDDEN = 'hidden',
    DOTTED = 'dotted',
    DASHED = 'dashed',
    SOLID = 'solid',
    DOUBLE = 'double',
    GROOVE = 'groove',
    RIDGE = 'ridge',
    INSET = 'inset',
    OUTSET = 'outset'
}

values.description_fallback: No border is drawn.
Border hidden — same as none but takes precedence in table border-collapse.
A series of rounded dots.
A series of short dashes.
A single solid line.
Two parallel solid lines.
A carved-in grooved effect.
A raised ridge effect — opposite of groove.
Makes the element appear embedded in the page.
Makes the element appear to protrude from the page.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { BORDER_STYLE } from 'origam'

const style: BORDER_STYLE = BORDER_STYLE.DASHED

entry.name: BOTTOM_NAV_POSITION
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for horizontal placement of the BottomNav bar when it does not span full width (start, center, end).

values.description_fallback: Align the bottom nav to the inline-start (left in LTR) of the layout region.
Center the bottom nav horizontally in the layout region.
Align the bottom nav to the inline-end (right in LTR) of the layout region.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { BOTTOM_NAV_POSITION } from 'origam'

const position: BOTTOM_NAV_POSITION = BOTTOM_NAV_POSITION.CENTER

entry.name: BRACKET_MATCH_STATUS
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for the status of a match in a tournament bracket (pending, live, completed, forfeited).

values.description_fallback: Match has not started yet.
Match is currently in progress.
Match has finished with a determined result.
Match was forfeited — one or both participants did not compete.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { BRACKET_MATCH_STATUS } from 'origam'

const status: BRACKET_MATCH_STATUS = BRACKET_MATCH_STATUS.LIVE

entry.name: BRACKET_VARIANT
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for the tournament bracket format (single-elimination, double-elimination, round-robin).

values.description_fallback: Single-elimination — one loss eliminates a participant.
Double-elimination — two losses required to eliminate a participant.
Round-robin — every participant plays against every other participant.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { BRACKET_VARIANT } from 'origam'

const format: BRACKET_VARIANT = BRACKET_VARIANT.SINGLE_ELIMINATION

entry.name: BREAKPOINTS
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for responsive breakpoint tiers used in the grid system (xs, sm, md, lg, xl, xxl).
entry.definition:
export enum BREAKPOINTS {
    XS = 'xs',
    SM = 'sm',
    MD = 'md',
    LG = 'lg',
    XL = 'xl',
    XXL = 'xxl'
}

values.description_fallback: Extra small — targets viewports below the sm threshold.
Small — targets small viewports (typically >= 600px).
Medium — targets medium viewports (typically >= 960px).
Large — targets large viewports (typically >= 1280px).
Extra large — targets extra-large viewports (typically >= 1920px).
Double extra large — targets the widest viewports (typically >= 2560px).

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { BREAKPOINTS } from 'origam'

const breakpoint: BREAKPOINTS = BREAKPOINTS.MD

entry.name: CALENDAR_STRATEGY
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for the rendering strategy of the date picker calendar (dynamic, static).

values.description_fallback: Dynamic — the calendar renders on demand inside a floating overlay.
Static — the calendar is permanently visible inline, not in an overlay.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { CALENDAR_STRATEGY } from 'origam'

const strategy: CALENDAR_STRATEGY = CALENDAR_STRATEGY.STATIC

entry.name: CARD_TYPE
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for the layout type of a Card inside a collection (grid, list).

values.description_fallback: Grid layout — card rendered in a multi-column grid with equal-height tiles.
List layout — card rendered in a single-column list with horizontal content flow.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { CARD_TYPE } from 'origam'

const type: CARD_TYPE = CARD_TYPE.GRID

entry.name: CHART_BULLET_ORIENTATION
entry.description_fallback: Orientation of a bullet chart — horizontal (progress bar style) or vertical (column style).

values.description_fallback: Bullet bar runs left to right.
Bullet bar runs bottom to top.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { CHART_BULLET_ORIENTATION } from 'origam'

const orientation = CHART_BULLET_ORIENTATION.HORIZONTAL

entry.name: CHART_CARTESIAN_KIND
entry.description_fallback: Chart topology selector for the cartesian family (line, area, bar, column, scatter, spline, stepped-line).
entry.definition:
export enum CHART_CARTESIAN_KIND {
    LINE = 'line',
    AREA = 'area',
    BAR = 'bar',
    COLUMN = 'column',
    SCATTER = 'scatter',
    SPLINE = 'spline',
    STEPPED_LINE = 'stepped-line'
}

values.description_fallback: Standard line chart connecting data points.
Area chart — fills the region below the line.
Horizontal bar chart.
Vertical column chart.
Scatter plot — individual data points without connecting lines.
Smooth curved line using spline interpolation.
Step line chart — data transitions as horizontal then vertical segments.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { CHART_CARTESIAN_KIND } from 'origam'

const kind = CHART_CARTESIAN_KIND.SPLINE

entry.name: CHART_HONEYCOMB_COLOR_MODE
entry.description_fallback: Color encoding mode for the honeycomb chart — categorical (one color per series) or heatmap (gradient intensity).

values.description_fallback: Each series gets a distinct categorical color.
Cell color encodes a continuous intensity value via a gradient scale.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { CHART_HONEYCOMB_COLOR_MODE } from 'origam'

const colorMode = CHART_HONEYCOMB_COLOR_MODE.HEATMAP

entry.name: CHART_HONEYCOMB_ORIENTATION
entry.description_fallback: Hexagon orientation for the honeycomb chart — pointy-top or flat-top.

values.description_fallback: Hexagons are oriented with a vertex pointing upward.
Hexagons are oriented with a flat edge at the top.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { CHART_HONEYCOMB_ORIENTATION } from 'origam'

const orientation = CHART_HONEYCOMB_ORIENTATION.FLAT_TOP

entry.name: CHART_MAP_MODE
entry.description_fallback: Rendering mode for the map chart — choropleth (region fill by value) or flight routes (arc connections).

values.description_fallback: Geographic regions are colored based on a data value.
Arcs connect origin and destination points on the map.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { CHART_MAP_MODE } from 'origam'

const mode = CHART_MAP_MODE.CHOROPLETH

entry.name: CHART_PICTORIAL_DIRECTION
entry.description_fallback: Fill direction for the pictorial bar chart — vertical (bottom to top) or horizontal (left to right).

values.description_fallback: Symbol fills from bottom to top.
Symbol fills from left to right.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { CHART_PICTORIAL_DIRECTION } from 'origam'

const direction = CHART_PICTORIAL_DIRECTION.VERTICAL

entry.name: CHART_PICTORIAL_MODE
entry.description_fallback: Symbol rendering mode for the pictorial chart — stack (repeat symbols) or fill (clip a single symbol).

values.description_fallback: Multiple symbol instances are stacked to represent the value.
A single symbol instance is clipped proportionally to the value.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { CHART_PICTORIAL_MODE } from 'origam'

const mode = CHART_PICTORIAL_MODE.FILL

entry.name: CHART_POLAR_KIND
entry.description_fallback: Chart type selector for the polar family — pie (full circle segments) or donut (ring with a hollow center).

values.description_fallback: Full pie chart — segments fill the entire circle.
Donut chart — segments form a ring around a hollow center.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { CHART_POLAR_KIND } from 'origam'

const kind = CHART_POLAR_KIND.DONUT

entry.name: CHART_PYRAMID_KIND
entry.description_fallback: Shape variant for the pyramid/funnel chart — pyramid (widest at bottom, tip up) or funnel (widest at top, narrows down).

values.description_fallback: Classic pyramid shape, widest segment at the base.
Funnel shape, widest segment at the top narrowing downward.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { CHART_PYRAMID_KIND } from 'origam'

const kind = CHART_PYRAMID_KIND.FUNNEL

entry.name: CHART_SPARKLINE_KIND
entry.description_fallback: Chart type for inline sparkline widgets — line, area, column, or bar.

values.description_fallback: Inline sparkline rendered as a line.
Inline sparkline rendered as a filled area.
Inline sparkline rendered as vertical columns.
Inline sparkline rendered as horizontal bars.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { CHART_SPARKLINE_KIND } from 'origam'

const kind = CHART_SPARKLINE_KIND.AREA

entry.name: CHART_STACKING
entry.description_fallback: Stacking strategy for bar, column, and area charts — normal (absolute values) or percent (normalized to 100%).

values.description_fallback: Series are stacked using their raw absolute values; the Y-axis spans 0 to max(stack total).
Each stack is normalised to 100%. The Y-axis is fixed at 0–100 and the tooltip shows both the raw value and its share.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { CHART_STACKING } from 'origam'

const stacking = CHART_STACKING.PERCENT

entry.name: CHART_STREAMGRAPH_OFFSET
entry.description_fallback: Baseline offset strategy for the streamgraph chart — wiggle, silhouette, expand, or zero.

values.description_fallback: Minimises the sum of squared slopes; produces the classic stream shape.
Centers the stream around zero, giving a symmetric silhouette.
Normalises values so the total at each point fills the full height (0–1).
All streams are stacked from a fixed zero baseline.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { CHART_STREAMGRAPH_OFFSET } from 'origam'

const offset = CHART_STREAMGRAPH_OFFSET.WIGGLE

entry.name: CHART_TREEMAP_ALGORITHM
entry.description_fallback: Layout algorithm for the treemap chart — squarified (aspect-ratio optimized) or slice-dice (alternating horizontal/vertical splits).

values.description_fallback: Recursively partitions space to produce rectangles with aspect ratios as close to 1 as possible.
Alternates horizontal and vertical splits at each depth level.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { CHART_TREEMAP_ALGORITHM } from 'origam'

const algorithm = CHART_TREEMAP_ALGORITHM.SQUARIFIED

entry.name: CHART_TYPE
entry.description_fallback: Master enum of all chart types supported by the Origam chart component family.
entry.definition:
export enum CHART_TYPE {
    LINE = 'line',
    AREA = 'area',
    BAR = 'bar',
    COLUMN = 'column',
    PIE = 'pie',
    DONUT = 'donut',
    SCATTER = 'scatter',
    RADAR = 'radar',
    SPLINE = 'spline',
    STEPPED_LINE = 'stepped-line',
    GAUGE = 'gauge',
    PYRAMID = 'pyramid',
    FUNNEL = 'funnel',
    HONEYCOMB = 'honeycomb',
    TREEMAP = 'treemap',
    SANKEY = 'sankey',
    WORD_CLOUD = 'word-cloud',
    HEATMAP = 'heatmap',
    SUNBURST = 'sunburst',
    BOX_PLOT = 'box-plot',
    PICTORIAL = 'pictorial',
    CANDLESTICK = 'candlestick',
    STREAMGRAPH = 'streamgraph',
    VARIWIDE = 'variwide',
    POLAR_BAR = 'polar-bar',
    BULLET = 'bullet',
    PARETO = 'pareto',
    MAP = 'map',
    SPARKLINE = 'sparkline'
}

values.description_fallback: Standard line chart.
Area chart with filled region.
Horizontal bar chart.
Vertical column chart.
Full pie chart.
Donut chart (pie with hollow center).
Scatter plot.
Radar / spider chart.
Smooth curved line chart.
Step line chart.
Gauge chart (speedometer style).
Pyramid chart.
Funnel chart for conversion steps.
Hexagonal honeycomb chart.
Treemap layout chart.
Sankey flow diagram.
Word cloud visualization.
Heatmap grid chart.
Sunburst hierarchical chart.
Box-and-whisker plot.
Pictorial bar chart using symbols.
OHLC candlestick chart.
Streamgraph (flowing area chart).
Variwide chart with variable column widths.
Polar bar chart (radial bars).
Bullet chart for performance vs. target.
Pareto chart (bars + cumulative line).
Geographic map chart.
Compact inline sparkline widget.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { CHART_TYPE } from 'origam'

const type = CHART_TYPE.TREEMAP

entry.name: CHART_WORD_CLOUD_ROTATION
entry.description_fallback: Word rotation strategy for the word cloud chart — none (horizontal only), random (any angle), or orthogonal (0° and 90° only).

values.description_fallback: All words are rendered horizontally (0°).
Words are rotated at random angles.
Words are rendered at either 0° or 90°.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { CHART_WORD_CLOUD_ROTATION } from 'origam'

const rotation = CHART_WORD_CLOUD_ROTATION.ORTHOGONAL

entry.name: CLIENT_POSITION
entry.description_fallback: Axis selector for pointer / touch event client coordinates (clientX or clientY).

values.description_fallback: Vertical axis — maps to the clientY property of a pointer event.
Horizontal axis — maps to the clientX property of a pointer event.

examples.title_fallback: Using CLIENT_POSITION to read the correct axis
examples.code:
import { CLIENT_POSITION } from 'origam'

const axis: CLIENT_POSITION = CLIENT_POSITION.X
const value = event[axis] // event.clientX

entry.name: CODE_LANG
entry.description_fallback: Supported syntax-highlighting languages for OrigamCode. Each value maps to a shiki grammar bundle shipped with the component.
entry.definition:
export enum CODE_LANG {
    PLAINTEXT = 'plaintext',
    VUE = 'vue',
    TS = 'ts',
    JS = 'js',
    TSX = 'tsx',
    JSX = 'jsx',
    SCSS = 'scss',
    CSS = 'css',
    JSON = 'json',
    BASH = 'bash',
    HTML = 'html',
    XML = 'xml',
    YAML = 'yaml',
    MD = 'md'
}

values.description_fallback: No syntax highlighting — raw plain text.
Vue SFC syntax (template, script, style blocks).
TypeScript.
JavaScript.
TypeScript with JSX.
JavaScript with JSX.
SCSS (Sass).
CSS.
JSON.
Bash / shell script.
HTML.
XML.
YAML.
Markdown.

examples.title_fallback: Rendering a TypeScript snippet
examples.code: <origam-code :lang="CODE_LANG.TS" :code="snippet" />

entry.name: CODE_THEME
entry.description_fallback: Controls which shiki colour theme OrigamCode applies. AUTO follows the host page data-theme attribute or prefers-color-scheme.

values.description_fallback: Follows the host page theme (data-theme or prefers-color-scheme).
Forces the light shiki theme regardless of the page theme.
Forces the dark shiki theme regardless of the page theme.

examples.title_fallback: Forcing dark highlighting
examples.code: <origam-code :theme="CODE_THEME.DARK" :lang="CODE_LANG.TS" :code="snippet" />

entry.name: COLOR_MODES_NAMES
entry.description_fallback: Color model identifiers used by OrigamColorPicker to switch between input representations (hex, rgb, hsl, …).
entry.definition:
export enum COLOR_MODES_NAMES {
    RGB = "rgb",
    RGBA = "rgba",
    HSL = "hsl",
    HSLA = "hsla",
    HEX = "hex",
    HEXA = "hexa"
}

values.description_fallback: Red / Green / Blue (no alpha).
Red / Green / Blue / Alpha.
Hue / Saturation / Lightness (no alpha).
Hue / Saturation / Lightness / Alpha.
Hexadecimal (6-digit, no alpha).
Hexadecimal with alpha channel (8-digit).

examples.title_fallback: Restricting the colour picker to hex input
examples.code: <origam-color-picker :mode="COLOR_MODES_NAMES.HEX" />

entry.name: COLS
entry.description_fallback: Grid column span values (1–12) used by the OrigamCol component to set its width within a 12-column grid.
entry.definition:
export enum COLS {
    ONE = 1,
    TWO = 2,
    THREE = 3,
    FOUR = 4,
    FIVE = 5,
    SIX = 6,
    SEVEN = 7,
    EIGHT = 8,
    NINE = 9,
    TEN = 10,
    ELEVEN = 11,
    TWELVE = 12,
}

values.description_fallback: Span 1 column (8.33 %).
Span 2 columns (16.67 %).
Span 3 columns (25 %).
Span 4 columns (33.33 %).
Span 5 columns (41.67 %).
Span 6 columns (50 %).
Span 7 columns (58.33 %).
Span 8 columns (66.67 %).
Span 9 columns (75 %).
Span 10 columns (83.33 %).
Span 11 columns (91.67 %).
Span 12 columns — full width (100 %).

examples.title_fallback: Using COLS for a half-width column
examples.code: <origam-col :cols="COLS.SIX">Content</origam-col>

entry.name: COVER_POSITION
entry.description_fallback: Side of the audio surface where the album cover is painted relative to the controller column in OrigamAudio.

values.description_fallback: Album cover is displayed to the left of the controller.
Album cover is displayed to the right of the controller.

examples.title_fallback: Placing the cover on the right
examples.code: <origam-audio :cover-position="COVER_POSITION.RIGHT" src="/track.mp3" />

entry.name: CROSS_ORIGIN
entry.description_fallback: Maps to the crossorigin HTML attribute for media elements (img, video, audio). Controls CORS credential behaviour when fetching the resource.

values.description_fallback: Sends a CORS request without credentials (cookies, certificates).
Sends a CORS request including credentials (cookies, client certificates).

examples.title_fallback: Loading an image from a CDN with CORS
examples.code: <origam-img :cross-origin="CROSS_ORIGIN.ANONYMOUS" src="https://cdn.example.com/photo.jpg" />

entry.name: DATATABLE_SELECT_STRATEGY
entry.description_fallback: Defines how rows are selected in OrigamDataTable: single row, current page, or all rows across pages.

values.description_fallback: Only one row can be selected at a time.
The header checkbox toggles all rows on the current page.
The header checkbox toggles all rows across every page.

examples.title_fallback: Enabling single-row selection
examples.code:
<origam-data-table
    :select-strategy="DATATABLE_SELECT_STRATEGY.SINGLE"
    :items="rows"
/>

entry.name: DATE_MODE
entry.description_fallback: Active view of OrigamDatePicker: day grid within a month, month list for the year, or year list for decade navigation.

values.description_fallback: Day-grid view — displays individual days of the selected month.
Month-list view — displays months of the selected year.
Year-list view — displays a range of years for decade navigation.

examples.title_fallback: Opening the picker in year-selection view
examples.code: <origam-date-picker :mode="DATE_MODE.YEARS" v-model="selectedDate" />

entry.name: DENSITY
entry.description_fallback: Camelcase editorial update

values.description_fallback: Camel item description
Reduced padding — useful in tables and toolbars.
Increased padding — comfortable for forms and cards.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { DENSITY } from 'origam'

const myDensity: DENSITY = DENSITY.COMPACT

examples.title_fallback: Camel example
examples.code: DENSITY.DEFAULT

entry.name: DIMENSIONS
entry.description_fallback: Enumeration of the six dimension prop keys supported by IDimensionProps and consumed by useDimension().

    HEIGHT = 'height',
    MAX_HEIGHT = 'maxHeight',
    MAX_WIDTH = 'maxWidth',
    MIN_HEIGHT = 'minHeight',
    MIN_WIDTH = 'minWidth',
    WIDTH = 'width'
}

values.description_fallback: Maps to the height CSS property.
Maps to max-height.
Maps to max-width.
Maps to min-height.
Maps to min-width.
Maps to the width CSS property.

examples.title_fallback: Iterating over dimension keys in a composable

entry.name: DIRECTION
entry.description_fallback: Two-axis direction enum used by scrollable or swipeable components to constrain movement to horizontal or vertical.

values.description_fallback: Movement or layout flows along the X axis.
Movement or layout flows along the Y axis.

examples.title_fallback: Vertical tabs
examples.code:
<origam-tabs :direction="DIRECTION.VERTICAL">
    <origam-tab value="a">Tab A</origam-tab>
    <origam-tab value="b">Tab B</origam-tab>
</origam-tabs>

entry.name: EASING
entry.description_fallback: Material Design 3 easing curves used by animated components (Parallax, Overlay, TranslateScale, Field). Each value is a cubic-bezier() CSS function.

values.description_fallback: Standard curve — used for most transitions (ease in and out).
Decelerate curve — elements entering the screen (ease out).
Accelerate curve — elements leaving the screen (ease in).

examples.title_fallback: Applying a decelerate curve to a parallax
examples.code: <origam-parallax :easing="EASING.DECELERATE" src="/hero.jpg" />

entry.name: EMPTY_STATE_PRESET
entry.description_fallback: Closed set of semantic preset values for OrigamEmptyState. Each preset provides a predefined icon, title and description.
entry.definition:
export enum EMPTY_STATE_PRESET {
    NO_DATA = 'no-data',
    NO_RESULTS = 'no-results',
    ERROR = 'error',
    OFFLINE = 'offline',
    LOCKED = 'locked'
}

values.description_fallback: No items exist yet — prompts the user to create the first one.
A search or filter returned zero matches.
Data loading failed — shows an error state with a retry affordance.
No network connection — asks the user to check their connection.
Content is gated behind a plan or permission.

examples.title_fallback: Showing an empty search result
examples.code: <origam-empty-state :preset="EMPTY_STATE_PRESET.NO_RESULTS" />

entry.name: FILTERS_MODE
entry.description_fallback: Strategy used when multiple active filters are evaluated together on a collection. Determines whether an item must satisfy some or all conditions.

values.description_fallback: An item passes if it satisfies at least one active filter.
An item passes only if it satisfies every active filter.
Returns the union of all per-filter result sets.
Returns only items present in every per-filter result set.

examples.title_fallback: Requiring all conditions to match
examples.code: <origam-select :filter-mode="FILTERS_MODE.EVERY" :items="options" />

entry.name: FLEX_DIRECTION
entry.description_fallback: CSS flex-direction values exposed as a TypeScript enum. Used by OrigamRow to control the main axis of a grid row.

values.description_fallback: Items are laid out left to right (default).
Items are stacked top to bottom.
Items are stacked bottom to top.
Items are laid out right to left.

examples.title_fallback: Reversing a grid row
examples.code:
<origam-row :direction="FLEX_DIRECTION.ROW_REVERSE">
    <origam-col :cols="6">Left (visually right)</origam-col>
    <origam-col :cols="6">Right (visually left)</origam-col>
</origam-row>

entry.name: FOCUS_LOCATION
entry.description_fallback: Enum describing focus navigation directions within a component (next, prev, first, last).

values.description_fallback: Move focus to the next focusable item.
Move focus to the previous focusable item.
Move focus to the first focusable item.
Move focus to the last focusable item.

examples.title_fallback: Using FOCUS_LOCATION in script setup
examples.code:
import { FOCUS_LOCATION } from 'origam'

const direction: FOCUS_LOCATION = FOCUS_LOCATION.NEXT

entry.name: IMG_STATE
entry.description_fallback: Lifecycle states for the OrigamImg component (idle, loading, loaded, error).

values.description_fallback: Image has not yet started loading.
Image is currently being fetched.
Image loaded successfully.
Image failed to load.

examples.title_fallback: Using IMG_STATE in script setup
examples.code:
import { IMG_STATE } from 'origam'

const state: IMG_STATE = IMG_STATE.LOADING

entry.name: INFINITE_SCROLL_MODE
entry.description_fallback: Trigger mode for OrigamInfiniteScroll: intersection-observer or manual button.

values.description_fallback: Load more items automatically when the sentinel enters the viewport.
Load more items only when the user clicks the load-more button.

examples.title_fallback: Using INFINITE_SCROLL_MODE in template

entry.name: INFINITE_SCROLL_SIDE
entry.description_fallback: Which side(s) of the scroll container can trigger loading (start, end, both).

values.description_fallback: Trigger at the beginning of the scroll list.
Trigger at the end of the scroll list.
Trigger at both the start and end of the scroll list.

examples.title_fallback: Using INFINITE_SCROLL_SIDE in template
examples.code:
import { INFINITE_SCROLL_SIDE } from 'origam'

const side = INFINITE_SCROLL_SIDE.BOTH

entry.name: INFINITE_SCROLL_STATUS
entry.description_fallback: Current fetch status emitted by OrigamInfiniteScroll (ok, empty, loading, error).

values.description_fallback: Data was loaded successfully; more items may be available.
No more items to load.
A fetch is currently in progress.
The last fetch failed.

examples.title_fallback: Using INFINITE_SCROLL_STATUS in script setup

entry.name: INLINE
entry.description_fallback: Horizontal anchor axis values (left, right) used for overlay positioning.

values.description_fallback: Anchor or position element to the left.
Anchor or position element to the right.

examples.title_fallback: Using INLINE in script setup
examples.code:
import { INLINE } from 'origam'

const side: INLINE = INLINE.RIGHT

entry.name: INLINE_EDIT_ACTION
entry.description_fallback: Identifies action buttons rendered by OrigamInlineEdit (edit, confirm, cancel).

values.description_fallback: Activates the inline edit mode.
Confirms and saves the inline edit value.
Cancels the inline edit and reverts the value.

examples.title_fallback: Using INLINE_EDIT_ACTION for data-cy attributes

entry.name: JUSTIFY
entry.description_fallback: Horizontal alignment / justify-content values for flex/grid containers.
entry.definition:
export enum JUSTIFY {
    START = 'start',
    END = 'end',
    CENTER = 'center',
    SPACE_BETWEEN = 'space-between',
    SPACE_AROUND = 'space-around',
    SPACE_EVENLY = 'space-evenly'
}

values.description_fallback: Pack items toward the start of the axis.
Pack items toward the end of the axis.
Center items along the axis.
Distribute items with equal space between them.
Distribute items with equal space around each.
Distribute items so all gaps are equal.

examples.title_fallback: Using JUSTIFY in script setup
examples.code:
import { JUSTIFY } from 'origam'

const align: JUSTIFY = JUSTIFY.CENTER

entry.name: KEYBOARD_MODIFIERS_KEY
entry.description_fallback: Keyboard modifier key identifiers used for hotkey configuration (ctrl, shift, alt, meta, cmd).
entry.definition:
export enum KEYBOARD_MODIFIERS_KEY {
    CONTROL = 'ctrl',
    SHIFT = 'shift',
    ALT = 'alt',
    META = 'meta',
    COMMAND = 'cmd'
}

values.description_fallback: The Control (Ctrl) modifier key.
The Shift modifier key.
The Alt (Option on macOS) modifier key.
The Meta key (Windows key on PC, Command on macOS).
Alias for the Command (Cmd) key on macOS.

examples.title_fallback: Defining a hotkey with modifier

entry.name: KEYBOARD_VALUES
entry.description_fallback: Standard KeyboardEvent.key values used for hotkey detection and keyboard navigation.
entry.definition:
export enum KEYBOARD_VALUES {
    ENTER = 'Enter',
    TAB = 'Tab',
    DELETE = 'Delete',
    ESC = 'Escape',
    SPACE = 'Space',
    UP = 'ArrowUp',
    DOWN = 'ArrowDown',
    LEFT = 'ArrowLeft',
    RIGHT = 'ArrowRight',
    END = 'End',
    HOME = 'Home',
    BACKSPACE = 'Backspace',
    INSERT = 'Insert',
    PAGEUP = 'PageUp',
    PAGEDOWN = 'PageDown',
    SHIFT = 'Shift',
    EMPTY = ' '
}

values.description_fallback: The Enter key.
The Tab key.
The Delete key.
The Escape key.
The Space key.
The Arrow Up key.
The Arrow Down key.
The Arrow Left key.
The Arrow Right key.
The End key.
The Home key.
The Backspace key.
The Insert key.
The Page Up key.
The Page Down key.
The Shift key.
A single space character, used as an empty key sentinel.

examples.title_fallback: Listening to a keyboard event

entry.name: LINES
entry.description_fallback: Number of text lines displayed per list item (one, two, three).

values.description_fallback: Display only one line of text per item.
Display up to two lines of text per item.
Display up to three lines of text per item.

examples.title_fallback: Using LINES in script setup
examples.code:
import { LINES } from 'origam'

const lines: LINES = LINES.TWO

entry.name: LIST_ITEM_TYPE
entry.description_fallback: Discriminates between the three possible children of OrigamList: item, subheader, or divider.

values.description_fallback: A standard interactive or display list item.
A non-interactive label that groups items beneath it.
A visual separator between list sections.

examples.title_fallback: Supplying typed items to a list
examples.code:
import { LIST_ITEM_TYPE } from 'origam'

const items = [
    { type: LIST_ITEM_TYPE.SUBHEADER, title: 'Group A' },
    { type: LIST_ITEM_TYPE.ITEM, title: 'Item 1' },
    { type: LIST_ITEM_TYPE.DIVIDER },
    { type: LIST_ITEM_TYPE.ITEM, title: 'Item 2' },
]

entry.name: LOCATION_STRATEGIES
entry.description_fallback: Positioning strategy for floating overlays: static (document flow) or connected (anchored to an activator).

values.description_fallback: The overlay stays in the document flow without anchoring.
The overlay is positioned relative to its activator element.

examples.title_fallback: Choosing a positioning strategy
examples.code:
import { LOCATION_STRATEGIES } from 'origam'

const strategy: LOCATION_STRATEGIES = LOCATION_STRATEGIES.CONNECTED

entry.name: MODE
entry.description_fallback: Scroll / expand axis for components like InfiniteScroll and Transition (horizontal, vertical, shift).

values.description_fallback: Operate along the horizontal axis.
Operate along the vertical axis.
Shift mode — translates content diagonally or based on pointer delta.

examples.title_fallback: Using MODE in script setup
examples.code:
import { MODE } from 'origam'

const scrollMode: MODE = MODE.VERTICAL

entry.name: OPEN_STRATEGY
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum controlling how tree/list nodes open — single, multiple, or list mode.

values.description_fallback: Only one node can be open at a time — opening a new one closes the previous.
Any number of nodes can be open simultaneously.
List mode — nodes open as a flat sequential list.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { OPEN_STRATEGY } from 'origam'

const strategy: OPEN_STRATEGY = OPEN_STRATEGY.SINGLE

entry.name: OTP_INPUT_FIELD_TYPE
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for OTP input field type — text, number, or password.

values.description_fallback: Plain text input — characters are visible as the user types.
Numeric input — triggers a numeric keyboard on mobile devices.
Password input — characters are masked for security.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { OTP_INPUT_FIELD_TYPE } from 'origam'

const fieldType: OTP_INPUT_FIELD_TYPE = OTP_INPUT_FIELD_TYPE.NUMBER

entry.name: PARALLAX_DIRECTION
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for Parallax scroll/motion direction — vertical, horizontal, or both axes.

values.description_fallback: Effect applies along the vertical (Y) axis only.
Effect applies along the horizontal (X) axis only.
Effect applies along both X and Y axes simultaneously.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { PARALLAX_DIRECTION } from 'origam'

const dir: PARALLAX_DIRECTION = PARALLAX_DIRECTION.VERTICAL

entry.name: PARALLAX_EASING
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for Parallax easing curve — linear, ease-out, or spring physics.

values.description_fallback: No easing — the element moves at a constant rate relative to scroll.
Deceleration easing — the element slows down as it reaches its target position.
Spring physics — the element overshoots slightly and settles with a natural bounce.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { PARALLAX_EASING } from 'origam'

const easing: PARALLAX_EASING = PARALLAX_EASING.SPRING

entry.name: PARALLAX_ELEMENT_TYPE
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for the CSS transform type applied to each Parallax element — scale, translate, rotate, depth, or custom.
entry.definition:
export enum PARALLAX_ELEMENT_TYPE {
    SCALE = 'scale',
    SCALE_X = 'scaleX',
    SCALE_Y = 'scaleY',
    TRANSLATE = 'translate',
    ROTATE = 'rotate',
    DEPTH = 'depth',
    DEPTH_INV = 'depth_inv',
    CUSTOM = 'custom'
}

values.description_fallback: Applies a uniform scale transform on both axes.
Applies a horizontal scale transform (scaleX).
Applies a vertical scale transform (scaleY).
Applies a translate transform — moves the element along the scroll axis.
Applies a rotation transform driven by scroll/motion progress.
Simulates depth — combines translateZ and scale to produce a 3-D perspective shift.
Inverted depth — element appears to recede as the user scrolls forward.
Custom transform — consumer provides a transform function via the element config.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { PARALLAX_ELEMENT_TYPE } from 'origam'

const elementType: PARALLAX_ELEMENT_TYPE = PARALLAX_ELEMENT_TYPE.TRANSLATE

entry.name: PARALLAX_EVENT
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for the browser event driving the Parallax effect — mouse move, scroll, or device orientation.

values.description_fallback: Effect is driven by the mousemove event — element tracks the pointer position.
Effect is driven by the scroll event — element moves with page scroll progress.
Effect is driven by the deviceorientation event — element reacts to gyroscope tilt (mobile).

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { PARALLAX_EVENT } from 'origam'

const trigger: PARALLAX_EVENT = PARALLAX_EVENT.SCROLL

entry.name: POSITION
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for CSS position strategy — static, relative, fixed, absolute, or sticky.
entry.definition:
export enum POSITION {
    STATIC = 'static',
    RELATIVE = 'relative',
    FIXED = 'fixed',
    ABSOLUTE = 'absolute',
    STICKY = 'sticky'
}

values.description_fallback: Default flow position — top/left/right/bottom/z-index have no effect.
Element is offset relative to its normal position in the flow.
Element is removed from flow and positioned relative to the viewport.
Element is removed from flow and positioned relative to its closest positioned ancestor.
Element is treated as relative until it crosses a defined scroll threshold, then fixed.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { POSITION } from 'origam'

const pos: POSITION = POSITION.STICKY

entry.name: PROGRESS_TYPE
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for Progress indicator display type — circular or linear.

values.description_fallback: Renders a spinning ring (spinner) for indeterminate or percentage progress.
Renders a horizontal bar filling left-to-right for percentage progress.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { PROGRESS_TYPE } from 'origam'

const progressType: PROGRESS_TYPE = PROGRESS_TYPE.CIRCULAR

entry.name: REFERRER_POLICY
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for the HTML referrerpolicy attribute — controls how much referrer information is sent with image requests.
entry.definition:
export enum REFERRER_POLICY {
    NO_REFERRER = 'no-referrer',
    NO_REFERRER_WHEN_DOWNGRADE = 'no-referrer-when-downgrade',
    ORIGIN = 'origin',
    ORIGIN_WHEN_CROSS_ORIGIN = 'origin-when-cross-origin',
    SAME_ORIGIN = 'same-origin',
    STRICT_ORIGIN = 'strict-origin',
    STRICT_ORIGIN_WHEN_CROSS_ORIGIN = 'strict-origin-when-cross-origin',
    UNSAFE_URL = 'unsafe-url'
}

values.description_fallback: No Referer header is sent with requests.
Full URL sent to same-security-level origins; no Referer on downgrade (HTTPS → HTTP).
Only the origin (scheme + host + port) is sent, never the path or query string.
Full URL for same-origin requests; only the origin for cross-origin requests.
Full URL sent only to same-origin destinations; no Referer header cross-origin.
Origin sent when same security level; no Referer on downgrade.
Full URL for same-origin; origin only cross-origin; nothing on downgrade. Browser default.
Full URL always sent — leaks path and query string even cross-origin. Use with care.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { REFERRER_POLICY } from 'origam'

const policy: REFERRER_POLICY = REFERRER_POLICY.STRICT_ORIGIN_WHEN_CROSS_ORIGIN

entry.name: ROUNDED
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for corner radius tokens (x-small through x-large, shaped, shaped-invert).
entry.definition:
export enum ROUNDED {
    X_SMALL = 'x-small',
    SMALL = 'small',
    DEFAULT = 'default',
    MEDIUM = 'medium',
    LARGE = 'large',
    X_LARGE = 'x-large',
    SHAPED = 'shaped',
    SHAPED_INVERT = 'shaped-invert'
}

values.description_fallback: Extra small corner radius (2px).
Small corner radius (4px).
Component default radius — resolved from the component token.
Medium corner radius (8px).
Large corner radius (12px).
Extra large corner radius (16px).
Pill shape — fully rounded left/right ends.
Inverted pill — concave corners on the ends.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { ROUNDED } from 'origam'

const myRounded: ROUNDED = ROUNDED.SHAPED

entry.name: SCROLL_BEHAVIOR
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for Toolbar scroll behavior — hide, invert, collapse, elevate, or activate on scroll.
entry.definition:
export enum SCROLL_BEHAVIOR {
    HIDE = 'hide',
    INVERTED = 'inverted',
    COLLAPSE = 'collapse',
    ELEVATED = 'elevate',
    ACTIVE = 'active',
}

values.description_fallback: Toolbar slides out of view when the user scrolls down and reappears on scroll up.
Inverted hide — toolbar shows when scrolling down and hides when scrolling up.
Toolbar shrinks (collapses) its height as the user scrolls down.
Toolbar gains an elevation shadow when the page is scrolled away from the top.
Toolbar enters an active/highlighted state when the page is scrolled.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { SCROLL_BEHAVIOR } from 'origam'

const behavior: SCROLL_BEHAVIOR = SCROLL_BEHAVIOR.HIDE

entry.name: SCROLL_STRATEGIES
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for overlay scroll strategy — none, close, block, or reposition.

values.description_fallback: No scroll strategy — overlay stays visible and scroll is unrestricted.
Overlay closes automatically when the user scrolls.
Body scroll is blocked while the overlay is open.
Overlay repositions itself as the user scrolls to stay anchored to its target element.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { SCROLL_STRATEGIES } from 'origam'

const strategy: SCROLL_STRATEGIES = SCROLL_STRATEGIES.CLOSE

entry.name: SELECTED
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for tri-state selection — on, off, or indeterminate.

values.description_fallback: The item is fully selected.
The item is not selected.
The item is in a mixed/partial selection state — typically used for parent nodes in tree views.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { SELECTED } from 'origam'

const state: SELECTED = SELECTED.INDETERMINATE

entry.name: SELECT_STRATEGY
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for tree/list selection strategy — single-leaf, leaf, independent, single-independent, or classic.
entry.definition:
export enum SELECT_STRATEGY {
    SINGLE_LEAF = 'single-leaf',
    LEAF = 'leaf',
    INDEPENDENT = 'independent',
    SINGLE_INDEPENDENT = 'single-independent',
    CLASSIC = 'classic'
}

values.description_fallback: Only one leaf node can be selected at a time; parent nodes are not selectable.
Any number of leaf nodes can be selected; parent nodes are not selectable.
Each node is selected independently — selecting a parent does not affect its children.
Only one node can be selected at a time, regardless of type (leaf or branch).
Classic cascading selection — selecting a parent automatically selects or deselects all its children.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { SELECT_STRATEGY } from 'origam'

const strategy: SELECT_STRATEGY = SELECT_STRATEGY.CLASSIC

entry.name: SIZES
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for component size scale (x-small, small, default, large, x-large).
entry.definition:
export enum SIZES {
    X_SMALL = 'x-small',
    SMALL = 'small',
    DEFAULT = 'default',
    LARGE = 'large',
    X_LARGE = 'x-large'
}

values.description_fallback: Extra small — smallest size token rung.
Small — reduced footprint for inline contexts.
Default — the baseline size for the component.
Large — increased surface area.
Extra large — maximum size rung.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { SIZES } from 'origam'

const mySize: SIZES = SIZES.LARGE

entry.name: SLIDER_FIELD_VARIANT
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for SliderField visual variant — full form field, bare timer scrubber, or waveform audio scrubber.

values.description_fallback: Full form-field chrome — label, messages, prepend/append icons. Default variant.
Bare scrubber — no input chrome; hairline rail (2 px at rest, 4 px on hover/focus), thumb hidden until hover. Used for video timelines.
Like TIMER but adds a waveform background rendered from the peaks prop. Used for audio scrubbers.

entry.name: TEXTAREA_MODE

values.description_fallback: Legacy mode — uses a native <textarea> element with a raw string v-model.
Rich text mode — uses a contenteditable surface; v-model emits sanitised HTML or Markdown depending on the output prop.

entry.name: TEXTAREA_OUTPUT
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for the serialisation format of the rich-text textarea v-model — HTML or Markdown.

values.description_fallback: Emits sanitised HTML (allowlisted tags, attributes and URL schemes).
Emits CommonMark-flavoured Markdown (bold, italic, links, lists, headings, code). Underline is preserved verbatim.

entry.name: TEXTAREA_TOOLBAR_COMMAND
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for rich-text toolbar command identifiers — bold, italic, link, lists, headings, code, clear-format.
entry.definition:
export enum TEXTAREA_TOOLBAR_COMMAND {
    BOLD = 'bold',
    ITALIC = 'italic',
    UNDERLINE = 'underline',
    LINK = 'link',
    LIST_BULLET = 'list-bullet',
    LIST_ORDERED = 'list-ordered',
    HEADING_1 = 'heading-1',
    HEADING_2 = 'heading-2',
    HEADING_3 = 'heading-3',
    HEADING = 'heading',
    CODE_INLINE = 'code-inline',
    CLEAR_FORMAT = 'clear-format'
}

values.description_fallback: Toggle bold formatting on the selection.
Toggle italic formatting on the selection.
Toggle underline formatting on the selection.
Insert or edit a hyperlink on the selection.
Toggle an unordered (bullet) list.
Toggle an ordered (numbered) list.
Apply heading level 1 to the current block.
Apply heading level 2 to the current block.
Apply heading level 3 to the current block.
Generic heading command (cycles through heading levels).
Toggle inline code formatting on the selection.
Strip all formatting from the selection.

examples.title_fallback: Building a custom toolbar subset

import { TEXTAREA_TOOLBAR_COMMAND } from 'origam'

const toolbar = [
    TEXTAREA_TOOLBAR_COMMAND.BOLD,
    TEXTAREA_TOOLBAR_COMMAND.ITALIC,
    TEXTAREA_TOOLBAR_COMMAND.LINK,
]

entry.name: TEXT_FIELD_TYPE
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for the HTML input type attribute values supported by OrigamTextField and related field components.
entry.definition:
export enum TEXT_FIELD_TYPE {
    TEXT = 'text',
    NUMBER = 'number',
    EMAIL = 'email',
    PASSWORD = 'password',
    HIDDEN = 'hidden',
    SEARCH = 'search',
    TEL = 'tel',
    URL = 'url',

    // IMAGE
    IMAGE = 'image',

    // RANGE
    RANGE = 'range',

    // FILE
    FILE = 'file',

    // COLOR
    COLOR = 'color',

    // BUTTONS
    SUBMIT = 'submit',
    BUTTON = 'button',
    RESET = 'reset',

    // CHECKBOX & RADIO
    CHECKBOX = 'checkbox',
    RADIO = 'radio',

    // DATE & TIME
    TIME = 'time',
    DATETIME = 'datetime-local',
    DATE = 'date',
    MONTH = 'month',
    WEEK = 'week'
}

values.description_fallback: Single-line plain text input.
Numeric input with optional stepper controls.
Email address input — browser validates basic format.
Password input — characters are masked.
Hidden input — not rendered visually, used for form data.
Search input — may display a clear button in some browsers.
Telephone number input — shows numeric keyboard on mobile.
URL input — browser may validate format.
Image submit button.
Range slider input.
File picker input.
Colour picker input.
Form submit button.
Generic button (no default behaviour).
Form reset button — clears all fields to their default values.
Checkbox toggle input.
Radio button input.
Time input (HH:MM).
Date-and-time input (datetime-local).
Date input (YYYY-MM-DD).
Month and year input.
ISO week number input.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { TEXT_FIELD_TYPE } from 'origam'

const fieldType: TEXT_FIELD_TYPE = TEXT_FIELD_TYPE.EMAIL

entry.name: VALIDATE_ON
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for the validation trigger of form fields — blur, input, submit, and lazy variants.
entry.definition:
export enum VALIDATE_ON {
    BLUR = 'blur',
    INPUT = 'input',
    SUBMIT = 'submit',
    LAZY_BLUR = 'lazy blur',
    LAZY_INPUT = 'lazy input',
    LAZY_SUBMIT = 'lazy submit',
    BLUR_LAZY = 'blur lazy',
    INPUT_LAZY = 'input lazy',
    SUBMIT_LAZY = 'submit lazy',
    LAZY = 'lazy',
}

values.description_fallback: Validate when the field loses focus.
Validate on every keystroke / value change.
Validate only when the form is submitted.
Lazy + blur — defers the first validation until blur, then validates on each change.
Lazy + input — defers the first validation, then validates on each input event.
Lazy + submit — defers until first submit, then validates normally.
Alias of LAZY_BLUR — blur then lazy re-validation.
Alias of LAZY_INPUT — input then lazy re-validation.
Alias of LAZY_SUBMIT — submit then lazy re-validation.
Defers all validation until explicitly triggered (e.g. form submit).

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { VALIDATE_ON } from 'origam'

const trigger: VALIDATE_ON = VALIDATE_ON.BLUR

entry.name: VARIANT
entry.description_fallback: TypeScript enum for component visual variants (text, flat, elevated, tonal, outlined, plain, ghost).
entry.definition:
export enum VARIANT {
    TEXT = 'text',
    FLAT = 'flat',
    ELEVATED = 'elevated',
    TONAL = 'tonal',
    OUTLINED = 'outlined',
    PLAIN = 'plain',
    /**
     * Glassmorphism — translucent tint of the current text color plus a
     * backdrop-filter blur. Browsers without `backdrop-filter` get a
     * slightly stronger tint as the fallback (handled in the SCSS via
     * `@supports`). Pairs cleanly with `color="primary"` etc. — the
     * variant only owns the chrome, not the foreground color.
     */
    GHOST = 'ghost'
}

values.description_fallback: Text-only surface — no background fill.
Filled background with no elevation shadow.
Filled background with box-shadow elevation.
Subtle tonal fill derived from the intent color.
Border only — no background fill.
Minimal chrome — blends into any surface.
Glassmorphism — translucent with backdrop-filter blur.

examples.title_fallback: Using the enum in script setup
examples.code:
import { VARIANT } from 'origam'

const myVariant: VARIANT = VARIANT.TONAL

values.description_fallback: Input has a bottom border only — classic Material Design style.
Input has a filled background with a bottom border — Material filled style.
Elevated card-like input with no visible border at rest.
Input with a full surrounding border — the most common accessible style.
Borderless, backgroundless input — minimal chrome for inline editing.
